column12: Measurement condition : Clear; Mostly Cloudy , No Precipitation , Calm Wind
column13: Control is channel and appears clear. There is a large gravel bar exposed ~150m downstream, channels flows on both side.
column14: Moving boat StreamPro measurement in tethered ARCboat #167, ~ 8m d/s cableway. Navigation Reference: BT (BT: 25.369, GGA: 24.899, VTG: 24.706). Uncertainty - COV: 1.7%. Uncertainty - Total: 4.4%. No significant QREV messages. No QA/QC concerns. High confidence measurement.  @ Uncertainty: QRev Uncertainty Analysis (DS Mueller, 2016), 2-sigma value (1 x Uncertainty Value reported in *.xml File). @ DWL taken near sensor line, with stilling bucket. Depth of sensor at 15:25 = DWL (4.874m) - offset (4.064m) = Sensor reading (0.810m). Purged 100psi at increased feed (50psi), sounded great, saw what appeared to be unabstructed bubbles in river. ADCP by Moving Boat

column12: Measurement condition : Clear; Mostly Cloudy , No Precipitation ,
column13: No obvious changes visually.
column14: Measurement quality was poor due to measurement section, appears to have angle flow due to cablefox rope orientation in relation to river, large eddies make it hard to get good edge without tethered boat getting out of control and tech unsure of how to appropriately estimate edge distances. Measurement file had some kind of error, couldn't run through QRev due to missing files. discharge value from WinRiver.  Trusting measurement as the shift magnitude was around ~ -18% to -30% the two recent previous visits. ADCP by Moving Boat

column12: Measurement condition : Clear; Clear , No Precipitation , Light Wind Blowing Downstream
column13: Control is channel & appears clear. Gravel bar on LB is different from previous visits.
column14: Moving boat measurement (SP w/ARC boat #99 & FlyingFox) ~5m D/S of cableway. Some moderate standing waves in transect. Transect 002 discarded due to false start. Navigation Reference: GGA (BT: 111, GGA: 111, VTG: 112). Uncertainty - COV: 1.8%. Uncertainty - Total: 4.2%. No significant QREV messages (some missing data; unavoidable). No QA/QC concerns. Medium confidence measurement. @ Uncertainty: QRev Uncertainty Analysis (DS Mueller, 2016), 2-sigma value (1 x Uncertainty Value reported in *.xml File). @ DWL at sensor line.  Large surge observed over sensor.  No recent discernible HWM.  Depth of sensor at 12:59: DWL(5.473) - Offset(4.112) = 1.361m.  ADCP by Moving Boat

column12: Measurement condition : Ice; Mostly Cloudy , No Precipitation , Calm Wind
column13: River running slush ice, 8-12m shore ice on LB, ~5-7m Shore ice on LB, River 75% open at gauge. Hard to tell how much back water exists due to curve shift and unknown amount of anchor ice on control. Based on channel observations estimating 5-15% backwater condition. Channel very deep just off RB, Some bank erosion on RB just upstream of gauge.
column14: Ice Affected control condions, see control condition remarks. Tried Moving Boat mmnt but drifting slush was causing too much missing data. Range finder used for boat positioning for sxs mmnt. ~7m shore ice on LB, chipped 2.5m of ice off to get the boat in water and panels done closer to shore. @ Uncertainty: ISO method, 2-sigma value (1 x Uncertainty Value reported in *.xml File). @ ~8m shore ice over top of area orifice block located. Mid-section

column12: Measurement condition : Clear; Cloudy , No Precipitation , Calm Wind
column13: Large tree debris 30m d/s of gauge on RB. Signs of significant bank errosion d/s 200m on LB where winter mmnts normally made
column14: Reasonable Quality ADCP mmnt, used cablefox to move boat across river. Purged orifice line, at 09:13,no Gauge reset until later.Evidence of a lot of new sand/silt deposited along river bank. Water levels too high to retrive orifice block. If levels lower next visit consider lifting out block and repositioning. Large woody debris is still d/s 30-40m on RR ADCP by Moving Boat

column12: Measurement condition : Clear; Clear , No Precipitation , Moderate Wind Blowing Upstream
column13: Looks like significant bank errosion 30-50m u/s of gauge on RR, it appears flow patterns in front of gauge have shifted with the bank errosion. Large submerged debris (root ball)   30m d/s of gauge on RR, may be causing some BW.
column14: Large standing waves in mmnt section, used cablefox to move boat across channel. Gravel bar on RL completely flooded and flows measured close to bank to bank. .3-.4m standing waves and water speed ~3-4m/s in area where orifice block is located. ADCP by Moving Boat
GC: 0.000 HG: 5.675 Surge: 0.040  * The uncertainty value is equivalent to the surge value in eHSN.  DWL

column12: Measurement condition : Ice; Cloudy , Light Snow , Calm Wind
column13: Channel mostly open from gauge to at least 300m d/s, ~50-90% ice cover u/s of gauge.
column14: Wading mmnt made ~100m d/s of gauge in same location as January mmnt. .85-.95m snow on ground by gauge. Photo taken of orifice Block. River mostly open at gauge and d/s. Mid-section

column12: Measurement condition : Ice; Clear , No Precipitation , Light Wind Blowing Downstream
column13: Channel 75% open at gauge w/ 4-8m of ice on each edge. Anchor ice forming inchannel u/s and d/s of gauge.
column14: Small amount of ice on edges of mmnt section, anchor ice forming d/s of mmnt section. Gauge not reset. ~60cm snow in forest by gauge. Large ice shelf overhanging water by gauge. Freeze up HWM not done due to too much snow to find freezeup line at shore. Not a major problem as gauge reasonably accurate. Mid-section

column12: Measurement condition : Clear; Clear , No Precipitation , Calm Wind
column13: No ice present on river edges. Very close to waterlevel that river stops flowing in side channel of gravel bar on RL just DS of gauge. Large clump of tree debris still DS on RL but mostly out of the water.
column14: Good quality flying fox mmnt, ARC boat 99 w/ no batteries used as tethered boat River Water clear, little wave action around orifice location at this level. ADCP by Moving Boat

column12: Measurement condition : Clear; Clear , No Precipitation , Calm Wind
column13: Large clump of debris on R side of channel 20m d/s of cblwy, may be affecting flow at site
column14: Flying Fox mmnt with SP in ARC Boat.  Corrected M.G.H. includes Sensor Reset Correction of 0.007 and Gage Correction of 0.000 applied to Weighted M.G.H of 4.945 orifice line left unplugged after tank changed, some invalid data around 10:00 ADCP by Moving Boat

column12: Measurement condition : Clear; Clear , No Precipitation , Moderate Wind Blowing Downstream
column13: Root ball, and tree debris on R side of channel just d/s of cblwy. probably not causing backwater
column14: Small .2m standing wavesin mmnt section. All flow in one channel,no side channel flow around gravel bar on RL. RiverRay measuring just ove 50% at this stage Levels done u/s of boulder by orifice. ADCP by Moving Boat

column12: Measurement condition : Clear; Partly Cloudy , No Precipitation , Calm Wind
column13: No ice or snow present on river banks. River flow all in one channel and no water flowing around gravel bar across from the gauge.
column14: Standing waves in mmnt section.Used ARC boat as tethered boat and handled waves well. Levels done in cove near where orifice line enters the water.TL cam pulled. ADCP by Moving Boat

column12: Measurement condition : Ice; Clear , No Precipitation , Very Light Wind Blowing Cross Stream
column13: Channel mostly open at gauge, ~2-4m edge i on LB, .5-1m edge ice on RB at gauge. Channel mostly ice covered u/s of gauge and mostly open d/s of gauge. WL below bottom of ice along RB for the most part.
column14: Mmnt section mostly ice free, with less than 1m shore ice on each bank. Bottom rocks getting slippery due to silt deposition and slime growth. Levels done directly by orifice block, water clear, photo taken of orifice block. Mid-section

column12: Measurement condition : Ice; Clear , No Precipitation , Calm Wind
column13: Channel mostly open at gauge, u/s, and d/s. ~1m edge ice on RB, 4-5m edge ice on LB at gauge. Anchor ice forming in rapids d/s of gauge, could  be causing some BW due to less channel area in control
column14: Anchor ice forming in mmnt section. Small trickle of water in side channel beside mmnt section, water may be coming out of bank or ground, no snow or ice deposited in area, Estimating 0.005 to 0.010cms, not significant, not includd in mmnt. No anchor ice forming in orifice area. Pulled TL cam. No HW freeze up surveyed, ice line not visible under snow. 1 to 1.1m snow at site. Mid-section

column12: Measurement condition : Clear; Partly Cloudy , No Precipitation , Calm Wind Blowing Downstream
column13: .3m edge ice on LB across from gauge, .2-.5m edge ice on LB by gauge, less ice on both sids of channel 50m d/s by control. Flowing slush across entire channel. Close to control clear, maybe slight backwater due to edge ice.
column14: SE 2021/01/29: ERROR WITH MGH ON FIELD NOTE. ERROR ONLY CORRECTED IN AQ AND NOT ON NOTE (CHANGED MGH FROM 4.578 TO 4.586).   Flowing slush in mmnt sect, slush ice soft and ~.1m thick flowing. Current vibrating FT2 sensor in centre of mmnt sect, hard to keep it level on some panels. Orifice block visible, looks good, no debris or silt on it. River flowing slush ice bank to bank, small amount of edge ice on both banks. Mid-section

column12: Measurement condition : Clear; Cloudy , Light Rain , Moderate Wind Blowing Downstream
column13: No Water flowing on other side of gravel bar across from gauge, water flowing around both sides of gravel bar d/s of gauge on RR.
column14: Mmnt made with cablefox and ARC 99 used as tethered boat Worked well in standing waves. ~.15-.2m standing waves in mmnt sect. River all in one channel in mmnt section at this stage. Levels done in cove d/s of orifice line. Water colour milky green. ADCP by Moving Boat

column12: Measurement condition : Clear; Mostly Cloudy , Light Rain , Moderate Wind Blowing Upstream
column13: Occasional drifting debris, 4 large trees came down the river after Qmmnt completed, Gravel bar d/s of gauge on RR completely submerged. Small amount (~0.5cms) water starting to flow over gravel bar and into side channel imediately accross from gauge under cableway, gravel bar not completely submerged.
column14: Used flying fox to pull ARC boat accross, .3-.4m standing waves in mmnt section.  Probably would be better to use cablefox for mmnts with more flow than this one, tethered boat was being grabbed and pulled forcefully d/s by current. Occasional drifting debris, 4 large trees came down the river just after mmnt completed. Large back eddy on RL side of mmnt created by upstream gravel bar, tethered boat brought to within edge of flowing water and not into eddy to avoid loss of boat control. Minimal amount of water flowing over gravel bar on RL (~.3-.5cms). Added an extra meter to shore estimate on Left side to account for unmeasured flow. Wavey conditions for compass calibration, not very good but GPS data looks reliable. No flat place that wasn't close to steel objects to set the boat up to spin w/ lazy susan.  Moving bed looks to be concentrated on L side of channel, very noticable from MB test and comparrison of BT and GPS data.  ~15cm standing waves in orifice location and water speed ~2m/s  ADCP by Moving Boat

column13: River mostly open at gauge.
column14: Old offset 3.955m, new offset 3.943m. River mostly open at gauge. ~10-15m shore/edge ice on LB, 2-3m edge ice on RB. Ice pushed up 2m on both banks form previous freeze up event.
